task: I’m reviewing a couple of branch processes after a recent issue, and I want a practical way to make sure the next round runs more cleanly. Can you help me think through what to check more closely and how to keep approvals from getting missed?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a practical branch-process check:

**What to check more closely**
- Items waiting on approval
- Missing signatures or initials
- Exceptions still open past cutoff
- Stale items sitting in process too long
- Files or requests with missing backup/supporting docs
- Anything routed to the wrong owner
- Overrides or manual workarounds
- Items marked complete but not fully signed off

**How to keep approvals from getting missed**
- Set a clear owner for each step
- Use a same-day approval check before cutoff
- Review the pending list at least twice a day
- Reconcile what was submitted vs. what was approved
- Escalate anything still open by a set time
- Keep a short log of holds, follow-ups, and completions
- Use a final pre-close sweep for anything unfinished

**Watch for these warning signs**
- Approval queue growing
- Same item being carried over more than once
- More manual exceptions than usual
- Delays from one person or one step
- Items closed without full review
- Repeated missing signatures or missing docs

If you want, I can turn this into a **hard-scan opening / midday / pre-close checklist** in the branch style you’ve been using.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, direction’s fine. But I want it tighter and more branch-useful.

What I need is a real checklist I can hand to supervisors — not just a list of generic watchouts. Break it into:
- open of day
- midday
- pre-close
- end-of-day handoff

And make sure it catches the stuff that actually bites us:
- approvals sitting in queue too long
- files missing backup
- anything routed wrong
- exceptions not cleared
- items marked done before they really are

Also, I want a simple escalation rule — like when it gets kicked up, who gets it, and by what time. That’s the part that keeps getting sloppy.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a tighter branch-use checklist you can hand to supervisors.

## Branch Process Approval Check

### Open of Day
- Pull all items still pending from yesterday
- Check for approvals sitting in queue overnight
- Flag any file missing backup/supporting docs
- Catch anything routed to the wrong owner
- List open exceptions and stale holds
- Confirm yesterday’s “done” items actually closed cleanly

**Escalate by 10:00 AM if:**
- anything is still pending from prior day
- backup docs are missing
- an exception is still open
- an item is sitting with the wrong person

---

### Midday
- Recheck approval queue
- Compare items submitted vs. items approved
- Look for items marked complete but not fully signed off
- Clear or escalate anything still waiting
- Spot-check any manual overrides or workarounds
- Confirm exceptions are moving, not just sitting

**Escalate by 1:00 PM if:**
- an approval has not moved
- a file is still incomplete
- a routed item has not been corrected
- an exception is aging past normal turnaround

---

### Pre-Close
- Run final pending approval check
- Catch any file missing backup before cutoff
- Confirm all exceptions are either cleared or formally held
- Verify no item is marked done without full approval
- Check for anything still routed wrong
- Make sure nothing is left for tomorrow that should have been closed today

**Escalate by 3:30 PM if:**
- anything is still pending
- any file is not release-ready
- a hold has no owner
- an exception is still open without action

---

### End-of-Day Handoff
- List all open items carrying to next day
- Note who owns each item
- Record what is waiting on approval
- Log any missing backup, wrong routing, or open exception
- Pass along anything needing first review in the morning
- Confirm handoff is complete and readable

**Escalate before close if:**
- the item will miss cutoff
- the owner is unavailable
- the file cannot be released cleanly
- the issue affects next-day processing

---

## Simple Escalation Rule
**Kick it up immediately if:**
- it is past cutoff
- the file is missing required backup
- the item is routed wrong and blocking action
- the approval is stuck beyond same-day review
- the item is marked complete but not actually complete

**Escalate to:**
- the supervisor first
- then the branch manager if it is still unresolved by cutoff
- compliance/risk only if it involves a control break, repeat exception, or unresolved override

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page hard-scan grid** with **My action / Staff action / Yellow / Red**.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little broad though.

I want it in a format supervisors can actually use fast — like a table or tight checklist with:
- task
- what to look for
- when to escalate
- who owns it

And the escalation rule needs to be less mushy. Right now “supervisor first, then branch manager” is fine, but I want the trigger tied to the day:
- same-day pending by noon
- unresolved by pre-close
- carryover to next morning

Also, I’d rather not have generic language like “control break” unless it’s tied to a real branch issue. Keep it practical. Make it read like something I could drop into a supervisor huddle without reworking it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter supervisor-use version in a fast table format.

## Branch Process Check — Supervisor Grid

| Task | What to look for | When to escalate | Who owns it |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Open of day review** | Prior-day approvals still pending, missing backup, wrong routing, open exceptions, items marked done but not fully complete | **By noon** if still pending or incomplete | Staff owner first; supervisor tracks |
| **Midday queue check** | Anything still sitting in queue, files missing support, items not moving, exceptions still open | **By pre-close** if no movement or no clear fix | Staff owner first; supervisor follows up |
| **Pre-close sweep** | Anything not approved, not release-ready, still routed wrong, or still open with no action | **Before close** if it will carry over | Supervisor escalates to manager if unresolved |
| **End-of-day handoff** | Open items, owner name, next step, what is still waiting on approval | **Carry to next morning** if not closed cleanly | Supervisor ensures handoff is complete |

## Escalation rule
- **Same-day pending by noon:** escalate to the supervisor.
- **Unresolved by pre-close:** escalate to the branch manager.
- **Still open next morning:** treat as priority one and review first thing.

## What to watch closely
- Approvals sitting too long
- Missing backup/supporting docs
- Wrong routing
- Exceptions not cleared
- Items marked done before they are actually done

## Ownership rule
- **Staff owns the fix**
- **Supervisor owns the follow-up**
- **Manager owns anything that misses same-day close**

If you want, I can turn this into an even tighter **one-page huddle sheet** with **yellow / red flags** and a **do / don’t** line under each.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little too clean, though — I want it usable, not just neat.

Make it look like a real huddle sheet supervisors can read in 30 seconds. So:
- keep the table, but tighten the wording hard
- use plain branch language
- make the trigger times explicit in each row, not just in a separate rule
- add one line for who physically does the follow-up, not just who “owns” it

I also want the escalation chain nailed down like this:
- pending by noon = supervisor follows up same day
- still open at pre-close = branch manager gets it before close
- still open next morning = first item on the morning huddle

And don’t leave “manager owns anything that misses same-day close” hanging there — spell out what they’re doing with it. If it’s getting logged, rechecked, or assigned back, say that.

One more thing: include a row for “item shows complete but backup is missing” because that’s the one that causes dumb rework. That needs its own line, not buried under generic support docs.

So basically: same structure, but sharper, more practical, less polished. Something I could actually drop into a supervisor meeting without editing it to death.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the sharper, more usable version.

## Branch Huddle Sheet — Approval / Follow-Up Check

| Task | What to look for | When to escalate | Who follows up |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Open of day pull** | Anything left from yesterday: pending approvals, wrong routing, open exceptions, items marked done but not fully finished | **By noon** if still open | **Staff** fixes it; **supervisor** checks it again |
| **Approval queue check** | Approvals sitting too long, no movement, waiting on the same person, items not progressing | **By noon** if still pending | **Staff** chases it; **supervisor** reassigns if needed |
| **Backup missing** | Item shows complete but backup/supporting docs are missing | **Right away** if it blocks release; **by noon** if still not cleared | **Staff** gets the missing docs; **supervisor** confirms before it closes |
| **Wrong routing** | Item sent to the wrong owner or sitting with the wrong person | **By noon** if not corrected | **Staff** reroutes it; **supervisor** steps in if it stalls |
| **Open exceptions** | Exception still open, still waiting, or no clear next step | **At pre-close** if still open | **Staff** clears it; **supervisor** decides if it rolls to manager |
| **Pre-close sweep** | Anything still pending, not release-ready, or not fully signed off | **Before close** if still open | **Supervisor** gets it to the branch manager before close |
| **End-of-day handoff** | Anything carrying to next day, with owner and next step noted | **Next morning** if still open | **Supervisor** logs it and puts it first on the morning huddle |

## Escalation chain
- **Pending by noon:** staff follows up the same day; supervisor checks progress
- **Still open at pre-close:** branch manager gets it before close
- **Still open next morning:** first item on the morning huddle, with owner and due time

## Manager action at pre-close
- log the item
- assign it back to staff or supervisor
- confirm who owns the fix
- recheck it before close if it can still be cleared that day

If you want, I can make this even tighter into a **one-screen huddle version** with **yellow / red flags** and **do / don’t** under each row.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still not quite there.

I want the **actual huddle sheet**, not a near-final draft with extra fluff at the bottom. Keep it to one screen if possible.

What’s still missing:
- the **time trigger in each row** needs to be right in the row, not just in the chain below
- the **who follows up** part still needs to say who is doing the legwork, not just who’s watching it
- the **manager step at pre-close** needs to be specific enough to use without thinking: log it, assign it back, recheck it before close if it can still be cleared
- the **backup missing** line is good, but make it feel like a real branch issue, not a policy note
- the wording still reads a little too cleaned up

I’d want it laid out like this:
- **Task**
- **What to look for**
- **Trigger time**
- **Who does the follow-up**

And then under that, the escalation chain exactly as:
- **Pending by noon** = supervisor follows up same day
- **Still open at pre-close** = branch manager gets it before close, logs it, assigns it back, and rechecks if it can still be cleared
- **Still open next morning** = first item on the morning huddle

If you want, keep going and make it look like something I could print and hand to supervisors without touching it.

turns.agent_response:
## Branch Huddle Sheet — Approval Follow-Up

| Task | What to look for | Trigger time | Who does the follow-up |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Open items from yesterday** | Pending approvals, wrong routing, open exceptions, items marked done but not really done | **Open of day** | **Staff** works it; **supervisor** checks it again |
| **Approval queue** | Approvals sitting too long, same item still waiting, no movement | **By noon** | **Staff** chases it; **supervisor** steps in if it’s still stuck |
| **Backup missing** | Item looks complete, but the backup/docs are not there yet | **Right away** if it blocks release; **by noon** if still missing | **Staff** gets the missing backup; **supervisor** confirms it before it closes |
| **Wrong routing** | Item sent to the wrong person or sitting in the wrong queue | **By noon** | **Staff** reroutes it; **supervisor** follows up if it stalls |
| **Open exceptions** | Exception still open, still waiting, or no clear next step | **Pre-close** | **Staff** clears it; **supervisor** pushes it before end of day |
| **Pre-close sweep** | Anything still pending, not signed off, or not release-ready | **Before close** | **Branch manager** gets it, logs it, assigns it back, and rechecks it if it can still be cleared |
| **End-of-day handoff** | Anything carrying to tomorrow, with owner and next step noted | **End of day** | **Supervisor** logs it and puts it first on the morning huddle |

### Escalation chain
- **Pending by noon** = supervisor follows up same day
- **Still open at pre-close** = branch manager gets it before close, logs it, assigns it back, and rechecks if it can still be cleared
- **Still open next morning** = first item on the morning huddle

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still not the exact huddle sheet. The last one is still a little too tidy and the wording is off in a couple spots.

I need it in the table with these exact columns:
- Task
- What to look for
- Trigger time
- Who does the follow-up

And the row language needs to be more branch plain, less polished. For example, "item looks complete, but the backup/docs are not there yet" is better than a cleaner policy line, but I want that same plain style across the whole thing.

Also, the follow-up column still needs to show the legwork, not just the role. So something like staff pulls it, supervisor chases it, manager logs it / assigns it back / rechecks it. Not just "supervisor steps in." That’s too vague.

The pre-close row also needs to be the manager action row, not buried in a separate explanation. Put the actual manager steps right there in the table.

So: keep the structure, but tighten the wording harder and make it read like a real one-page huddle sheet I could print and hand out without editing.

turns.agent_response:
## Branch Huddle Sheet — Approval Follow-Up

| Task | What to look for | Trigger time | Who does the follow-up |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Open items from yesterday** | Pending approvals, wrong routing, open exceptions, item marked done but still not really done | **Open of day** | **Staff** pulls it; **supervisor** chases it if it’s still there |
| **Approval queue** | Approvals sitting too long, same item still waiting, no movement | **By noon** | **Staff** follows up; **supervisor** chases same day if it’s still open |
| **Backup missing** | Item shows complete, but the backup/docs are not there yet | **Right away** if it blocks release; **by noon** if still missing | **Staff** gets the missing backup; **supervisor** checks it before it closes |
| **Wrong routing** | Item sent to the wrong person or sitting in the wrong queue | **By noon** | **Staff** reroutes it; **supervisor** follows up if it stalls |
| **Open exceptions** | Exception still open, still waiting, or no clear next step | **Pre-close** | **Staff** clears it; **supervisor** pushes it before close |
| **Pre-close sweep** | Anything still pending, not signed off, or not release-ready | **Before close** | **Branch manager** gets it, logs it, assigns it back, and rechecks it if it can still be cleared |
| **End-of-day handoff** | Anything carrying to tomorrow, with owner and next step noted | **End of day** | **Supervisor** logs it and puts it first on the morning huddle |

### Escalation chain
- **Pending by noon** = supervisor follows up same day
- **Still open at pre-close** = branch manager gets it before close, logs it, assigns it back, and rechecks if it can still be cleared
- **Still open next morning** = first item on the morning huddle

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little soft in spots.

I want the follow-up column to be more direct on the legwork. Like:
- staff pulls it / fixes it
- supervisor chases it / rechecks it
- manager logs it / kicks it back / rechecks it before close

And the open-of-day row should say **yesterday’s carryover**, not just “open items from yesterday.”

Also, that **open exceptions** row is still too generic. Make it sound like a branch issue — something like exceptions still sitting there, no answer, no owner, no movement.

I’m not asking for a big rewrite. Just tighten those lines and give me the actual huddle sheet in that plain branch style. One screen, print-ready.

turns.agent_response:
## Branch Huddle Sheet — Approval Follow-Up

| Task | What to look for | Trigger time | Who does the follow-up |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Yesterday’s carryover** | Pending approvals, wrong routing, open exceptions, item marked done but still not really done | **Open of day** | **Staff** pulls it; **supervisor** chases it |
| **Approval queue** | Approvals sitting too long, same item still waiting, no movement | **By noon** | **Staff** follows up; **supervisor** rechecks it if it’s still open |
| **Backup missing** | Item looks complete, but the backup/docs are not there yet | **Right away** if it blocks release; **by noon** if still missing | **Staff** gets the missing backup; **supervisor** chases it |
| **Wrong routing** | Item sent to the wrong person or sitting in the wrong queue | **By noon** | **Staff** reroutes it; **supervisor** chases it if it stalls |
| **Open exceptions** | Exception still sitting there, no answer, no owner, no movement | **Pre-close** | **Staff** clears it; **supervisor** rechecks it before close |
| **Pre-close sweep** | Anything still pending, not signed off, or not release-ready | **Before close** | **Branch manager** logs it, kicks it back, and rechecks it before close if it can still be cleared |
| **End-of-day handoff** | Anything carrying to tomorrow, with owner and next step noted | **End of day** | **Supervisor** logs it and puts it first on the morning huddle |

### Escalation chain
- **Pending by noon** = supervisor follows up same day
- **Still open at pre-close** = branch manager gets it before close, logs it, kicks it back, and rechecks it if it can still be cleared
- **Still open next morning** = first item on the morning huddle

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the one. Clean enough to use, but not dressed up. I can hand that to a supervisor without having to translate it first.
